tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting significant renal dysfunction, possibly nephrotic syndrome or acute/chronic kidney disease. Blood glucose is normal, but urine glucose and ketones are present, with a low HbA1c, which may indicate recent metabolic changes or renal glycosuria. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
